LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION AND PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. JUDICIAL POLICY FAVORS RESOLUTION OF ALL DISPUTED MATTERS BETWEEN THE PARTIES IN THE SAME LAWSUIT. THE COURT'S DISCRETION WILL BE EXERCISED LIBERALLY TO PERMIT AMENDMENT OF THE PLEADINGS. [SEE NESTLE V. SANTA MONICA (1972) 6 C3D 920, 939; MABIE V. HYATT (1998) 61 CA4TH 581, 596; HOWARD V. COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO (2010) 184 CA4TH 1422, 1428.] IN TORT CASES, PLAINTIFF MAY SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES FOR "OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE" BY DEFENDANT. [SEE CIV.C. SEC. 3294(A).] HERE, A TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND DEFENDANT PADOVER'S ACTIONS RISE TO THE LEVEL OF "MALICE" AND MAY FIND PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-15-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ATTY. SUMAIR S. SANDHU OF THE LAW OFFICE OF MCDOWELL SHAW GARCIA & MIZELL, TEL. NO. 1-916-564-9710 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Jamie Padover's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is DENIED in its entirety. Defendant's motion is procedurally defective and substantively fails. The court sustains plaintiff's objection to the declaration of Mr. Sandhu and the attached exhibits because he does not authenticate the attachments. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is defective because the separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350(d). Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production as to the causes of action for negligence, assault, intentional infliction of emotional distress and the claim for punitive damages. Defendant's motion lacks competent evidence and even if the court entertains defendant's adduced evidence, the motion still fails. The Amended Complaint alleges that defendant made the obscene gesture, stuck his arm out of his window and extended it such that it seemed that he intended to push plaintiff, and yelled at plaintiff. (Amended Complaint, pars. VI, IX-X, and XVI-XVIII.) Defendant selectively cites the record and ignores the allegation that he extended his arm in a threatening manner. That allegation, which defendant cites no evidence to disprove, supports all of the claims. Request for sanction was denied by the court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar May-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA REKOW'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is granted. Judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit. The court's discretion will be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. [See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 C3d 920, 939; Mabie v. Hyatt (1998) 61 CA4th 581, 596; Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 CA4th 1422, 1428.] In tort cases, Plaintiff may seek punitive damages for "oppression, fraud or malice" by Defendant. [See Civ.C. Sec. 3294(a).] Here, a trier of fact could find Defendant Padover's actions rise to the level of "malice" and may find punitive damages are appropriate. Order signed. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.
